segments.s011-g9: This is a small noun phrase headed by 'چیزی' (something) with a relative clause introduced by 'که' (that/which). 'به' at the start marks direction/goal ('to/for'), and 'باشد' is an optative/subjunctive present ('may be' / 'be') qualifying 'چیزی'. The enclitic object 'مرا' (written here split as م + را) marks 'me' as affected, and 'دسترس' (reach) is the noun completing the idea 'within my reach'.
segments.s011-g10: 'بکوشم' is a present-subjunctive/agentive form meaning 'I will try / I shall strive' (first‑person). The following phrase states the intended result: 'نیازت' = 'your need' as a possessive (نیاز + -ت). 'نیارم' here functions as a verb meaning 'I will not make/bring (it) to ...' (the exact lemma printed is inconsistent, so the sense is inferred from context). 'به کس' means 'to anyone' (direction/recipient).
segments.s011-g11: 'همی داشتم' is a habitual/past continuous expression meaning 'I was holding / I kept' (همی + past form داشتم). 'چون' introduces a simile 'like/as', and 'یکی تازه سیب' is a simile noun phrase 'a certain fresh apple' (یکی = a certain/one, تازه = fresh, سیب = apple). Word order is simile_marker + verb + like-phrase.
segments.s011-g12: The relative 'که' ('which/that') begins a clause. 'از باد' = 'from the wind' (باد as agent). The verb form 'نامد' is irregular/poetic here (contextually 'came' / 'was brought by') — the printed senses are not a direct match, so the exact lemma is inferred from context. 'به من' = 'to me', and 'بر نهیب' is a verbal/nominal phrase meaning 'struck upon (me)' or 'gave a sudden shock' (بر = upon; نهیب = a loud/striking sound or blow).
segments.s011-g13: 'به کیوان رسیدم' is a simple past clause: 'به' (to) + 'کیوان' (proper name) + 'رسیدم' (I arrived/reached). 'ز خاک نژند' is a poetic ablative 'from lowly dust' (ز = from, خاک = dust/earth, نژند = base/worthless) describing origin. The order is preposition + place + verb, then origin phrase.
segments.s011-g14: 'از' again marks origin/association ('from/of'), and the string 'آن نیک‌دل نامدار ارجمند' is a noun phrase in apposition: 'that kind‑hearted, famed, honorable one'. Each adjective follows the head demonstrative 'آن' and stacks as attributive modifiers. The whole phrase supplies who is meant by the preceding 'from the dust'.
segments.s011-g15: 'به چشمش' is an idiom meaning 'in his eyes / in his view' (به + چشم + -ش enclitic). 'همان خاک و هم سیم و زر' lists items in parallel: 'that very dust and also silver and gold' — 'همان' = that/same, and the repeated 'هم' gives 'also/even'. Word order is prepositional phrase (in his eyes) followed by the list of things regarded as equivalent.
segments.s011-g16: This clause says what the viewer perceived: 'کریمی بدو یافته' = 'he found generosity toward him' (کریمی = generosity / generousness or a generous person; بدو = to him; یافته = found/obtained). The final 'زیب و فر' are two nouns in apposition meaning 'beauty and royal glory' — objects/qualities that were discovered in the person. Word order is verb phrase + past participle + complements.
segments.s011-g17: سراسر جهان پیش او خوار بود is a nominal clause with the locative/contrastive phrase پیش او ('before/in the presence of him') placed before the predicate خوار بود ('was lowly/despised'). سراسر جهان is a preposed adverbial phrase meaning 'throughout the world'; خوار is a predicative adjective and بود is the past copula.
segments.s011-g18: جوانمرد بود و وفادار بود contains two predicative nominatives linked by و ('and'), each completed by the past copula بود. The repetition of بود after both predicates is a poetic, emphatic pattern (rather than using a single copula for both).
segments.s011-g19: چنان نامور گم شد از انجمن is a past-tense clause where چنان ('so/thus') modifies نامور ('renowned') and گم شد ('became lost/vanished') is the verb; از انجمن ('from the assembly') indicates the source/setting from which he disappeared. شد here is the past intransitive 'became/went'.
segments.s011-g20: چو در باغ سرو سهی از چمن is a simile introduced by چو ('as/like'); the phrase is elliptical (no main verb) and describes the manner of disappearance. در باغ ('in the garden') locates the image, سرو سهی is a noun + postpositive epithet (سهی, a poetic adjective 'dark/tall'), and از چمن ('from the lawn/grass') further situates or separates the cypress.
segments.s011-g21: نه ز او زنده بینم نه مرده نشان is a negated verbless/exclamatory construction with نه ... نه ('neither ... nor'). ز او ('of/from him') marks the person concerned; زنده بینم ('I see alive') and مرده نشان ('a sign/trace of him dead') are coordinated negative phrases — بینم is 1st-person present of دیدن 'to see'.
segments.s011-g22: به دست نهنگان مردم کشان is a prepositional/agent phrase: به دست ('into/in the power/hand of') governs the agent phrase نهنگان مردم‌کشان, where مردم کشان is a participial/adjectival phrase 'men-dragging' modifying نهنگان ('whales'). The whole phrase describes an imagined fate ('in the clutches of whales that drag people').
segments.s011-g23: دریغ آن کمربند و آن گردگاه uses دریغ as an exclamation ('pity/alas') followed by two coordinated definite demonstrative NPs (آن کمربند 'that girdle' and آن گردگاه 'that neck/neck‑ornament'). The demonstrative آن is repeated for emphasis before each noun, a common poetic device.
segments.s011-g24: دریغ آن کیی برز و بالای شاه repeats the exclamatory دریغ and applies it to abstract royal attributes: کیی here names the Kayanian (a royal/ dynastic epithet), and برز و بالای شاه are paired nouns meaning 'exaltedness/height and the high status of the king.' The syntax is nominal and elliptical (the verb is understood from the previous clause).
segments.s011-g1: بدین نامه چون دست کردم دراز = a temporally framed clause: بدین نامه (به + این) 'toward/at this letter', چون 'when' (archaic/poetic use), دست کردم 'I stretched/laid my hand' (کردم = past of کردن). دراز here is part of the verbal idea 'دست دراز کردن' (to reach out / stretch out one's hand) and follows the verb phrase in inverted poetic order.
segments.s011-g2: یکی مهتری بود گردن‌فراز = main clause with subject and predicate: 'یکی' = 'a certain one', مهتری 'a chief/elder' as the noun, بود = past copula 'was', and گردن‌فراز an epithet (adj.) 'proud / neck‑raising' placed after the noun in usual Persian adjective position.
segments.s011-g3: جوان بود و از گوهر پهلوان = 'he was young' (جوان بود, adjective + past copula), followed by a genitive/partitive phrase 'و از گوهر پهلوان' literally 'and of the essence/of the very nature of a champion' (از used to mark 'out of / of the very').
segments.s011-g4: خردمند و بیدار و روشن‌روان = a string of coordinating adjectives modifying the same subject: خردمند 'wise', بیدار 'awake/alert', روشن‌روان a compound epithet 'bright‑souled / clear‑minded'; the conjunction و simply links the qualities.
segments.s011-g5: خداوند رای و خداوند شرم = two honorific epithets: خداوند here functions as 'possessor/master' (an honorific, not the divine sense), followed by qualities رای 'counsel/plan' and شرم 'modesty' — i.e., 'master of counsel and master of modesty.'
segments.s011-g6: سخن گفتن خوب و آوای نرم = nominal phrase describing his manner: سخن گفتن 'speaking' (infinitive/gerund used as a noun) + خوب 'good' and coordinate phrase 'و آوای نرم' 'and a gentle voice' (آوا = voice, نرم = gentle). The phrase functions as predicate giving his communicative qualities.
segments.s011-g7: مرا گفت کز من چه باید همی = 'He said to me' (مرا before the verb is the classical word‑order marking the indirect object), followed by a relative/subordinate clause introduced by کز (archaic contraction of که از / 'that/from which') meaning roughly 'what is to be done by me'; چه باید همی = 'what ought to be (done)' with باید 'must/ought' and the archaic particle همی as an emphatic/presentive element.
segments.s011-g8: که جانت سخن برگراید همی = purpose/result clause introduced by که 'that/so that': جانت 'your soul/heart' is the beneficiary, سخن 'the speech/words', and برگراید (subjunctive/optative sense) 'may turn toward / be pleased by'. The final همی is the archaic modal/particle reinforcing the optative sense ('may indeed').
segments.s011-g25: گرفتار is used predicatively here as a past-participle/adjective meaning 'taken/afflicted' and is followed by the agent marker ز + او ('by him'). دل‌شده is another participial phrase 'having the heart made' (i.e. heart-struck/distraught) and ناامید is a simple adjective 'hopeless' modifying the same person; the three descriptors stack as coordinate predicates.
segments.s011-g26: This is a compact descriptive clause: نوان 'young/youthful' + a pair لرز (noun 'trembling') and لرزان (participle/adjective 'trembling, shaking') describe the person; به کردار uses به in an adverbial/manner sense 'in the manner of' and کردار 'behaviour/conduct', and بید is the simile 'willow' — together 'trembling in conduct like a willow'.
segments.s011-g27: یکی پند 'one admonition' is a noun phrase where یکی functions as 'a certain/one' and آن شاه qualifies the pند ('that king's advice'). یاد آوریم is a compound verb 'bring to mind/recall' in 1st-person plural (آوریم) used hortatively — 'let us/we recall'.
segments.s011-g28: ز کژی 'from crookedness' gives the source/state to be abandoned; روان 'the soul/mind' is the thing being turned; سوی داد آوریم is a verb phrase 'we bring/turn (it) toward justice' with سوی marking direction and آوریم (1pl) as the main verb 'bring'.
segments.s011-g29: مرا گفت is 'he said/told to me' with the object marker را attached (here split). کاین introduces a content clause 'that this ...' and نامه شهریار is the noun phrase 'the sovereign's letter' (نامه modified by شهریار). The clause is reported speech introduced by گفت.
segments.s011-g30: گرت is the archaic/poetic conditional 'if' (contraction of گر + ت or similar). گفته آید is a reported/passive-like construction 'it is/shall be said/reported (that...)' and the imperative/main advice follows: به شاهان سپار — سپار is a jussive/imperative 'entrust/hand over' with به شاهان marking the recipients 'to the kings'.
segments.s011-g31: بدین نامه is a prepositional phrase 'by/with this letter' (بدین = به + دین in older/poetic spelling), من is the subject 'I', and دست بردم فراز is a verbal phrase literally 'I put/laid my hand up/on high' — here idiomatically 'I wrote/inscribed (it) above' or 'I signed atop'. بردم is past tense (1st sing.) of بردن used with دست to mean 'laid/put (my hand)'.
segments.s011-g32: به نام is the formula 'in the name of' with به used adverbially to mark manner/formulaic use, شهنشاه is the archaic title 'Shahanshah' (king of kings), and گردن‌فراز is an epithet 'proud-necked' attached as a descriptive epithet of the sovereign.

sentences.s011-s5: I will strive to obtain whatever is within my reach so that I do not have to make your need depend on anyone.
sentences.s011-s6: I held it as one holds a fresh apple, which the wind brought and struck against me.
sentences.s011-s7: I arrived at Kiwan from base dust — from that kind‑hearted, renowned, and noble one.
sentences.s011-s8: In his eyes that same dust and even silver and gold were alike; he found in him generosity, beauty, and royal glory.
sentences.s011-s9: The whole world was lowly before him; he was a chivalrous man and was faithful.
sentences.s011-s10: Thus the renowned one vanished from the assembly, like a dark cypress in the garden apart from the grass.
sentences.s011-s11: I see neither him alive nor any sign of him dead; (as if) in the hands of whales that drag men.
sentences.s011-s12: Alas for that girdle and that collar; alas for that Kayanian loftiness and the high dignity of the king.
sentences.s011-s1: When I stretched out my hand toward this letter, a proud, high-ranking elder stood (there).
sentences.s011-s2: He was young, and in the very essence of a champion: wise, alert, and clear‑minded.
sentences.s011-s3: A master of counsel and a master of modesty; his speech was good and his voice gentle.
sentences.s011-s4: He said to me what I ought to do — so that your soul might be pleased by the words.
sentences.s011-s13: Captured/ensnared by him, heart-struck and hopeless, youthful, trembling and shaking in manner like a willow.
sentences.s011-s14: Let us recall one admonition of that king; we will turn the soul from crookedness toward justice.
sentences.s011-s15: He told me that this letter is the sovereign's; if it is said to you, hand it over to the kings.
sentences.s011-s16: On this letter I set my hand aloft; in the name of the Shahanshah, the proud-necked.
